titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the John Clayton Tracy Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Lisa Newman

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 10:07

did.abstract: The John Clayton Tracy Papers consist of writings, correspondence, and subject files relating to Tracy's role as professor of civil engineering and head of the Department of Civil Engineering at the Sheffield Scientific School, secretary of the Yale Class of 1890 (S.S.S.), and Yale representative to the Society of Sigma Xi.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mrs. Carleton Smith and the Department of Civil Engineering, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Clayton Tracy Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Clayton Tracy, civil engineer and author, was born in Willimantic, Connecticut on November 3, 1869. He received his Ph.B. from the Sheffield Scientific School, Yale University, in 1890, and his C.E. in 1892. Tracy was an instructor (1891-1892), assistant professor (1902-1915), and professor (1915-1936) in the Department of Civil Engineering at Yale. He also served as department chairman from 1915-1936.

Member of the Society for the Promotion of Engineering Education, Connecticut Society of Civil Engineers, Graduates Club. Director of the New Haven Chamber of Commerce, Member Executive Committee Yale Engineering Association, Yale representative on the national council of the Society of the Sigma Xi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The John Clayton Tracy Papers consist of writings, correspondence, and subject files relating to Tracy's role as professor of civil engineering and head of the Department of Civil Engineering at the Sheffield Scientific School, secretary of the Yale Class of 1890 (S.S.S.), and Yale representative to the Society of Sigma Xi.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: [Surveying; Theory and Practice or Plane Surveying?]: drafts and related papers

title.part: Plane Surveying: A Text Book and Pocket Manual

did.unittitle: [Draft of letter to McGraw Hill Co. about book?], advertisements
"Permissible Errors in Measurements on Running Tracks," related correspondence
"Talk to the Graduating Class of Civil Engineers"
"Notes for Lecture on the Panama Canal," miscellaneous papers
Correspondence of Tracy as Class Secretary
Papers relating to the forty-fifth reunion
"Class Notes, Alumni Magazine, including notices of deaths"
Papers on engineering education, miscellaneous papers
"Discussion of engineering problems at Yale," miscellaneous papers
"Plans for the Development of the Engineering Departmentat Yale University"
John C. Tracy: "'Notes on Bridge Design'...prepared for the use of the students..."
Papers and letters related to the Red Cross
Papers concerning the Tracy Memorial Tablet, miscellaneous papers
